title: loop.jsonl entry (.workflow/logs/run-<id>/loop.jsonl)
description: Dual-track logging: 'mechanical' entries are written by hooks/loop-log.sh (Stop hook), unconditionally, no model involvement. 'semantic' entries are written by the model itself per CLAUDE.md convention, one per iteration, sharing the same iteration number as its mechanical counterpart. 'run_summary' is appended once at run end by whichever step detects a stop signal.

mainSessionTokens.description: Sum of usage.{input,output,cache_creation_input,cache_read_input}_tokens from claude -p's --output-format json result. null means the run was NOT invoked with --output-format json — this is a real, expected gap (defaults to plain text with no usage data), not an error to paper over. Never fabricate a number here.

subagentTokensSum.description: Sum from token-tally.sh over the run's subagent_tokens.count file. 0 is a legitimate value (no subagents called).

totalTokens.description: mainSessionTokens + subagentTokensSum, or null if mainSessionTokens is null (in which case subagentTokensSum alone is a known lower bound, not the true total).

totalCostUsd.description: Rough order-of-magnitude estimate only: totalTokens * APPROX_USD_PER_TOKEN (see hooks/loop-log.sh or the skill's run_summary step for the current constant, default 0.000003 = $3/million tokens as of writing). Deliberately does NOT distinguish input/output/cache-write/cache-read pricing tiers, which differ by roughly 10x on Claude — this is for a quick 'is this in the right ballpark' check, not for billing or precise budget tracking. Use total_cost_usd from claude -p's --output-format json result instead when exactness matters. null if totalTokens is null (can't estimate without a token count).
